title: Start + End = Timeline
excerpt:
I have been trying to use the “Start + End = Timeline” App created by the Excellent Team. 

  
However, I have been having an issue where whenever I try to add recipes from this app, an error message (like the one I have…

posts.author: ryo (Ryosuke Aramaki)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have been trying to use the “Start + End = Timeline” App created by the Excellent Team.

monday.com

monday.com: Apps Marketplace

monday.com apps marketplace, Expand the capabilities of your Work OS with monday apps

スクリーンショット (392)1807×936 18 KB

However, I have been having an issue where whenever I try to add recipes from this app, an error message (like the one I have attached) shows up. The message states that you can not access this website.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @ryo

I am the developer of this app and hope we can find a solution. I’ll send you a message with some questions privately

posts.author: ryo (Ryosuke Aramaki)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @basdebruin
I really loved your app, I asked my company’s security team to deal with the issue.
In the mean time, I visited to your company’s website to send you a request regarding this app, and would be great if you could check them.
Thank you

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @ryo

I just saw the message but will also reply here so others also understand. With regards to your ERR_CONNECTION_RESET, it might be a hickup on my server side. I do only communicate with the monday servers, and it looks like a connection issue between your side and monday. I will investigate further.

With regards to multiple instances in the same board: I deliberately refused that because there is also a reverse option (from timeline to dates) and you can image what happens if a user put in 2 recipes where:

  1. one uses dates to timeline
  2. the other one does the reverse (using the same columns

This will become very messy :). So, it is not a bug, it is a feature

I do understand your use case and will try to make some changes to allow multiple recipes as long as you are using different columns. Will let you know.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ryo

I did some investigation and I can make a version that allows multiple recipes per board. I will need to check however if output columns of recipe 1 are not used as input to recipe 2. My apps do send data to my server on a “strictly need to know” basis. That implies that for the current version I don’t even know if you use it from dats to timeline or visa versa.

So, to make this happen, I need to change my database a little and change the recipes (probably with a direction switch). I can’t just change the existing recipes as this would be disruptive for all users.

I will give it a go, but probably need at lease 1-2 weeks to fully design, build and test it.

posts.author: valentineC (Valentine)
posts.cookedHtml:
Trying to use the Start + End = Timeline App but the automation I’m using doesn’t seem to be working.
I’m using the second formula:
When Date, Date or timeline changes, synchronize in the direction of.
I want any changes to my start date or my end date to be reflected to my timeline, however any changes to these dates column have no impact on the timeline column and I don’t understand why.

posts.author: rob (Roberto Tremonti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sorry for hijacking the thread, @valentineC.

I’m sure @basdebruin will help you in making the app work on your account.
Otherwise you can take a look at General Caster app, that lets you build a timeline using two dates.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: Already DM’d @valentineC as I need the accountURL to check the logfiles

posts.author: valentineC (Valentine)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @basdebruin

Apologies for the confusion. It appear the app had run out of the trial period. I paid the $34 fee and it now works like a charm. I just had one question about pricing: is it a one time fee or a subscription based fee? Also can all members of my organisation use it or do they also each have to pay that fee?

Thank you so much for your help!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Valentine,

No problem and thank you for your purchase. All our apps are priced as a one-time fee.

posts.author: Clynch (Craig Lynch)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @basdebruin

I’m getting an issue when trying to fit the standard automation onto a board. It says to cotact the developer… is the app still live?

Thanks

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @Clynch

Sure the app is live and kicking. Is there anything shown in your monday notification area? If not, please let me know your monday account URL so I can check the logfiles.

posts.author: Alberto (Alberto Borges)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bas!
I am also trying to use this app but I’m getting an error without any tip suggesting me to contact the developer team.
Could you please support?

Monday_error_start_end_timeline1810×801 25.8 KB

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @Alberto

Can you let me know your monday account URL (if you prefer send me a DM) so I can have a look at the log files.

posts.author: Alberto (Alberto Borges)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bas! It’s https://farfetch.monday.com/.
I believe I’ve just understood the root cause of this issue.
I was not aware that this integration app is payed and that’s probably the reason why this error is thrown. Btw, could you please clarify if the “one time payment” cost enables the app for all FF users (our the enterprise account) or if the payment is per user?
Thank you for your quick reply!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Alberto,

Glad you found the reason for this. The one-time prices listed on our site are per monday account (independent of nr. of users).
